campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026, where they discussed staff hiring challenges and recruitment efforts, heard public comments on various matters, and addressed upcoming community events including the Boogie Festival happening that weekend.
keyTopics: Staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention strategies
Public hearing on government code section 3502 matters
Boogie Festival this weekend
Public comment period on council reports

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 6, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council met to move forward on a financial assessment or improvement project by approving an engineer's report and scheduling an ordinance for adoption on May 20th. The council also explored whether Saratoga should become a charter city and reviewed preliminary tax and revenue options to help fund city services.
keyTopics: Engineer's Report approval for a fiscal year project
Public hearing and ordinance adoption scheduled for May 20, 2026
Exploring charter city status for Saratoga
Preliminary tax options and revenue estimates under review

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 16, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on June 16, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from recent sessions and authorize the Town Manager to sign several contracts and agreements. These included updates to existing software services, legal services, and litigation support arrangements.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes from June 2 and June 8, 2026
Authorized new five-year software services agreement
Approved amendments to legal services and litigation contracts
Adopted a resolution

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 23, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met on June 23, 2026, to discuss several major items including whether to protect a historic building at 647 South Sixth Street, a proposal to use ranked choice voting for mayoral elections, advertising rights at the airport, and a long-term power contract with Shell Energy North America.
keyTopics: Historic landmark designation and tax break contract for 647 South Sixth Street property
Potential change to election rules allowing ranked choice voting for mayor and council vacancies
Advertising sign contract award at San José airport
Long-term power purchase agreement with Shell Energy North America

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 23, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to discuss upcoming litigation cases, honor local organizations and a newly appointed judge, set the city's spending limits for the next fiscal year, and approve plans for water management, infrastructure projects, and environmental restoration. Key decisions included adopting updated water shortage plans and moving forward with capital improvement projects like parking lot repairs and salt pond restoration.
keyTopics: Two pending litigation cases requiring legal strategy discussion
Water management and drought contingency plans for fiscal year 2026-27
Capital Improvement Program budget and spending priorities
Public Safety Building construction project updates
Shoreline Golf Links parking lot repaving project

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 23, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-door meeting on June 23, 2026, followed by a regular council meeting at 7 P.M. The specific details of what was discussed in the closed session were not made public, as these meetings are held privately to discuss confidential matters.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting held at 6 P.M.
Regular council meeting convened at 7 P.M.

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — July 7, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a study session to review and update the city's Health and Safety Element, a key part of the long-range General Plan that guides development through 2040. The council also recognized the Parks and Recreation Department for their work across multiple programs and celebrated Monta Vista High School's Science Olympiad Team achievements.
keyTopics: Updating Health and Safety Element of the General Plan (2015-2040)
Recognition of Parks and Recreation Department divisions and programs
Monta Vista High School Science Olympiad Team achievement

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 30, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a hybrid meeting on June 30, 2026, allowing residents to participate either in person or online via Zoom. The council provided information about how residents can submit written comments before meetings using the eComment system.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format - attend in person or join via Zoom
How to submit written public comments before council meetings
Remote participation options for residents who cannot attend in person

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review several procedural and planning matters, including updates to the city's housing and community development plan for 2025-2030, new work requests from individual council members, and a proposed change to how council vacancies are filled. The council also approved meeting minutes and reviewed the calendar for upcoming sessions.
keyTopics: Substantial Amendment to 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan (housing and federal funding priorities)
Changes to Council Vacancy Ordinance (how empty council seats are filled)
New workplan item requests from individual councilmembers
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — June 10, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to approve a zoning change for a property at 3300 El Camino Real, adjusting the setback requirement (the distance a building must be set back from the street) along Hansen Way to 20 feet. The council also handled routine administrative matters like approving meeting schedules and planning commission meeting notes.
keyTopics: Zoning setback change at 3300 El Camino Real on Hansen Way to 20 feet
Planning & Transportation Commission meeting minutes from April 29, 2026
City Council schedule and assignments
